title: Deterministic scratch evidence review report
problem: Ticket-070 provides machine-readable scratch DB summaries, but operators and principals still need a single formatted evidence review artifact (markdown/JSON) for human review before seeding improvement tickets. Today that composition is manual.
acceptance_criteria: New CLI (e.g. probe-scratch-evidence-review) produces a deterministic evidence review from scratch DB via existing read-only summary builder.
Command reuses build_scratch_summary; does not duplicate aggregation logic or mutate scratch/accepted graph DBs.
Default prints formatted report to stdout; optional --out writes only under agent_reports/ or data/reports/.
Report includes fixture/floor/stage/rejection counts, latest report paths, and explicit safety attestation flags.
Report contains no raw prompts, model responses, operator note bodies, or automated ticket recommendations.
Missing scratch DB fails closed unless --allow-empty is passed through.
Empty valid scratch DB produces a valid zero-state review.
Deterministic output for identical scratch DB state.
Golden/mock gates and safety audit pass.
non_goals: No LLM, OpenAI, or cloud API.
No scratch or accepted graph DB writes.
No public export or public-site changes.
No Qwen/model ticket authority or auto-seeding from report text.
No changes to live probe commands or validators.
rollback_plan: Revert evidence review module, CLI subcommand, tests, and runbook section; probe-scratch-summary unchanged.
